title: Verifying the Architecture
concept: Checking output tensor shapes and parameter counts to confirm the architecture is correct; 'Next up: DL Project: Training Loop.'.

exercises.prompt: What do we get after passing input through the forward pass in a neural network?
exercises.explain: The output from the forward pass gives us the predictions or logits, which represents the model’s confidence in each class.

cards.title: Verifying the Architecture
cards.body: In this lesson, we will learn how to check the output shapes and parameter counts in our model to verify that it was built correctly.

cards.title: Output Tensor Shapes
cards.body: The output tensor shape tells us the dimensions of the data produced by our neural network. Correct shapes ensure the data flows as expected.

cards.title: Shapes Like Puzzle Pieces
cards.body: Think of output tensor shapes as puzzle pieces. They need to fit perfectly together for the whole puzzle (your model) to look right. Mismatched shapes can cause problems.

cards.title: Checking Shapes Example
cards.body: If our model is for image classification with 10 output classes, the output shape should be [batch_size, 10]. This tells us how many images we’re processing and how many classes we’re predicting.

cards.title: Myth: Shapes Don’t Matter
cards.body: Some might think that as long as the model runs, shapes don’t matter. This is false! Incorrect shapes can lead to errors or incorrect predictions.

cards.title: Comparing Shapes & Counts
cards.body: We can visualize the expected output shapes and parameter counts for model verification.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following should you verify to ensure your model architecture is correct?
exercises.explain: Verifying output tensor shapes ensures that data flows as expected through the model and matches our design.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Checking parameter counts isn’t important for verifying model architecture.
exercises.explain: False. Checking parameter counts is crucial as it helps confirm that your model complexity is as intended.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: We check the output __________ to confirm our neural network's architecture is working as expected.
exercises.sentence: We check the output tensor shapes to confirm our neural network's architecture is working as expected.
exercises.explain: We check the output tensor shapes to ensure that our model is producing results that match our expectations.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements about model verification is generated by the AI?

options.text: Output shapes help ensure model integrity.
The number of layers is most crucial in model tests.
Parameter counts can be ignored during checks.

exercises.explain: The AI correctly notes that output shapes help ensure model integrity, indicating the checks necessary for a functioning architecture.

exercises.prompt: What should you check to verify a neural network architecture?

options.text: Only the model's depth and width
Output tensor shapes and parameter counts

exercises.explain: You should check output tensor shapes and parameter counts to confirm that the architecture of your model is functioning as intended. This verification ensures that the model can handle data appropriately and is built correctly.
